weakest_eval.justification: Native <button>, <select>, <input>, <form>, <table>, <nav>, <ol>, <fieldset>, and <legend> are all used appropriately. Minor issue: the 'Place Order' <button type='submit'> in step 3 is not inside any <form> element, making the submit type semantically incorrect.

Mostly disciplined: <nav> elements are labelled, <section> and <aside> landmarks are labelled, aria-current is used correctly, aria-labelledby on 'Add to cart' buttons is a legitimate technique. One redundancy: role="search" on the <form> inside the <search> element is unnecessary because <search> already exposes the search landmark role.

Single h1 for the article title; h2 used correctly for article sections, 'About the author', 'Related Articles', and 'Comments'. However, 'Leave a comment' is also marked as h2 despite being a subsection nested inside the 'Comments' h2 section — it should be h3, creating a skipped-level inversion in that part of the outline. Related article and comment headings correctly use h3.

All expected landmarks are present and correctly placed: <header>, <nav aria-label="Main">, <search>, <aside>, <main id="main-content">, <footer>. Three skip links (content, search, filters) are the first focusable elements and appropriate for this page's complexity. <main> correctly excludes the site header, search bar, and footer. <section> in footer has aria-labelledby.

All inputs have visible labels, sub-groups within steps use <fieldset>/<legend> (Contact details, Delivery address, Credit card details), email error correctly uses aria-invalid='true' + aria-describedby, and autocomplete attributes are thorough. However, the key expectation that each top-level step be wrapped in a <fieldset> with <legend> is missed — steps use <section>/<h2> instead. Step 3 also has no <form> element wrapping the Place Order button.
Pricing, plan names, and feature values are realistic. Intro paragraph is natural. Company name is a generic placeholder ('Your Company').
Navigation items are in <ul> within <nav>. Product cards are in a <ul> (unordered, as sequence is not meaningful). Footer nav links are in <ul>. Correct list types throughout.
Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and <th scope='col'> on every header cell. <time datetime> used for dates. Table is used only for tabular data with no layout abuse.

expectations_missed: No ARIA attributes used where native HTML provides the same semantics — role="search" on the <form> inside the <search> element is redundant since <search> already provides the search landmark role
Each step uses <fieldset> with <legend> for grouping — steps use <section>/<h2> instead; fieldsets only appear for sub-groups within steps
Place Order button in step 3 has type='submit' but is not inside a <form> element
'Leave a comment' heading uses h2 despite being a subsection nested inside the 'Comments' h2 section — should be h3 to maintain hierarchy
Comment author names use <h3> rather than <strong>/<b> or <span> as the key expectations suggest; h3 is defensible as the comment article's heading but departs from the stated expectation
